aliases: Plain rice porridge with sweet potato

english: Plain rice porridge with sweet potato

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general congee class; no specific Teochew sweet potato porridge entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a Teochew-specific sweet potato porridge entry. A00LC (rice-based porridge) is the closest defensible code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for Teochew porridge with sweet potato; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 300 g (one bowl), as commonly consumed in Singapore as a light breakfast or supper dish.

nutrient_source.title: Teochew Porridge (Sweet Potato) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Teochew porridge (sweet potato), cr_id F05100139, with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this primary regional source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice porridge / congee analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Teochew sweet potato porridge. GI of 61 (medium) is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain rice congee / rice porridge, which reports GI values in the range 57–78 depending on rice variety and cooking duration. A value of 61 is used as a conservative mid-range estimate consistent with the high water dilution and gelatinised starch profile of Teochew-style porridge. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 30.06) / 100 ≈ 18.4 for a 300 g bowl.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely low sodium content (2 mg/100 g) with potassium contribution from sweet potato (~80 mg/100 g estimated)
hypertension.guidance: Teochew sweet potato porridge is suitable for individuals with hypertension when prepared without added salt or high-sodium condiments; the low sodium and modest potassium content support the DASH dietary pattern, but high-sodium accompaniments such as salted fish or preserved vegetables should be avoided.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (61) from gelatinised white rice starch, low dietary fibre (0.05 g/100 g), and 10 g carbohydrate per 100 g resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 18 per standard bowl
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a bowl and pair this porridge with protein-rich sides such as steamed fish, egg, or tofu to blunt the glycaemic response; increasing the proportion of sweet potato relative to white rice would improve fibre content and lower the overall GI.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.12 g/100 g), negligible saturated fat (~0.03 g/100 g), and very low sodium (2 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teochew sweet potato porridge is a heart-friendly staple due to its very low fat and sodium content; it is best consumed as part of a balanced meal that includes vegetables and lean protein to ensure adequate micronutrient and fibre intake for cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low dietary fibre (0.05 g/100 g) and absence of meaningful omega-3 fatty acids or polyphenols at this dilution level
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This porridge does not meet the anti-inflammatory dietary threshold on its own; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should add ginger or turmeric to the preparation and serve alongside oily fish to introduce anti-inflammatory compounds.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teochew Porridge with Sweet Potato (潮州粥（番薯）) is a traditional Singaporean Chinese dish originating from the Chaoshan (Teochew) culinary tradition of southern China. It is prepared by simmering white rice with chunks of orange-fleshed sweet potato in a large volume of water until the grains are fully softened but retain some individual grain structure, producing a thin, clear-broth porridge distinct from the thicker Cantonese-style congee. The texture is light and watery, with soft sweet potato pieces providing a mild natural sweetness and a slightly starchy mouthfeel. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 45 kcal, 0.85 g protein, 0.12 g fat, and 10.02 g carbohydrate, with very low sodium at 2 mg and minimal dietary fibre at 0.05 g, reflecting the high water dilution inherent to Teochew-style preparation. The glycemic index is estimated at 61 (medium) based on the gelatinised white rice starch profile, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 18 per standard 300 g bowl. Clinically, the dish is low in sodium and fat, making it suitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management, but its medium GI and low fibre content require portion control and protein-rich accompaniments for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. In Singapore, Teochew porridge is commonly served as a breakfast or light supper dish, accompanied by small side dishes such as braised tofu, steamed fish, preserved vegetables, or salted egg. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and halal in its plain form.
common_user_queries: Is Teochew porridge good for diabetics?
How many calories in Teochew sweet potato porridge?
What is the GI of rice porridge with sweet potato?
Can I eat Teochew porridge if I have high blood pressure?
What is 潮州粥 in English?
How much carbohydrate in sweet potato porridge?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05100139) with a reported confidence of 0.95. This is the primary approved regional source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for rice congee, clearly documented as an estimate.
notes: 1. sugar_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is a confirmed value from the primary source reflecting the absence of added sugars and the negligible free sugar content of the diluted sweet potato in this preparation. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, moisture, and micronutrient data for this specific Teochew sweet potato porridge preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F05100139 as of 2026-04-16. 3. saturated_fat_g (0.03 g) and potassium_mg (80 mg estimated) are derived from USDA FoodData Central analogous rice porridge and sweet potato entries as supplementary estimates, clearly flagged. 4. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain rice congee; no direct measurement for Teochew sweet potato porridge exists in the approved GI database. 5. Glycemic load per serving = (61 × 30.06) / 100 ≈ 18.4 for a standard 300 g bowl. 6. The rheumatoid arthritis caution referencing nightshades in the original input has been removed from the main health context as sweet potato (Ipomoea batatas) is not a nightshade (Solanaceae); it belongs to Convolvulaceae.
